compiled_state.title: As a DSO operator running automated pipelines, I can invoke preplanning non-interactively so it completes without blocking on human-input checkpoints
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add conditional branches at all 7 interactive checkpoints in `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` and wire the PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE flag in `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/prompts/ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md`. Read `preplanning.interactive` once at preplanning startup via `read-config.sh`, normalize to lowercase, and pass the flag through the workflow.

**Why**: Today preplanning blocks at 7 checkpoints waiting for human input. Automation agents (sprint, CI, multi-agent orchestration) stall indefinitely with no operator present. This story implements the non-blocking fallback behavior at each checkpoint.

**Scope**:
- IN: `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` (conditional branches at all 7 checkpoints); `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/prompts/ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md` (PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE wiring); tests for all 7 fallback behaviors
- OUT: Config infrastructure changes (S1); documentation (S3); onboarding (S4); non-interactive mode for other skills

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, invoking preplanning without an epic-id in non-interactive mode exits immediately with a clear, structured error message identifying that an epic-id is required
  ← Satisfies: "invocation without an epic-id exits immediately with a clear error message"
- When this story is complete, invoking preplanning when scope clarification is needed during reconciliation in non-interactive mode exits immediately with a clear error message identifying what needs clarification
  ← Satisfies: "scope clarification needed during reconciliation exits immediately with a clear error message"
- When this story is complete, escalation policy defaults to "Escalate when blocked" in non-interactive mode without prompting the operator
  ← Satisfies: "escalation policy defaults to 'Escalate when blocked' without prompting"
- When this story is complete, reconciliation approval is auto-applied in non-interactive mode, with a guard that prevents auto-approving Delete recommendations for stories currently in_progress status
  ← Satisfies: "reconciliation approval for existing children is auto-applied"
- When this story is complete, the story dashboard is suppressed and final story map approval is skipped in non-interactive mode, completing the workflow automatically and producing a story map
  ← Satisfies: "story dashboard is suppressed; final story map approval is skipped"
- When this story is complete, ui-designer dispatch (Phase 4 Step 6) uses INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED paths in non-interactive mode for all 7 checkpoints including the ui-designer dispatch protocol
  ← Satisfies: "ui-designer dispatch protocol (Phase 4 Step 6) uses INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED paths"
- When this story is complete, all 7 checkpoints behave identically to today's interactive behavior when `preplanning.interactive=true` (no regression)
  ← Satisfies: "preplanning.interactive=true preserves today's interactive behavior at all six checkpoints unchanged"
- When this story is complete, unit tests cover all 7 non-interactive checkpoint fallback behaviors including the Delete-in-reconciliation guard
  ← Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done requirement AND "tests exercising all six non-interactive checkpoint fallbacks pass"

## Considerations

- [Configuration] Read `preplanning.interactive` once at startup via `read-config.sh`; normalize to lowercase; pass as a parameter rather than re-reading at each checkpoint — avoids 7 separate `read-config.sh` calls
- [Configuration] Use `[[ "$PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE" == "true" ]]` (not `!= "false"`) — `read-config.sh` returns empty string for absent keys; treating empty string as non-interactive is the correct default
- [Reliability] Auto-approval of reconciliation in non-interactive mode must guard against Delete recommendations for in-progress stories — apply only to pending/open stories in the reconciliation list
- [Maintainability] The 7th checkpoint (ui-designer dispatch protocol) is in a separate file (`ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md`) — ensure PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE is passed through when the protocol is invoked

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
